purpose: Idle-power minimization experiment on the Jetson Orin Nano Dev Kit: since the edge classifier is ~10^-6 duty cycle at real event rates, board idle power is the entire deployment power budget. This measures how much of that idle is actually reducible by software configuration (desktop vs headless, 7W vs 15W nvpmodel).
method: VDD_IN board-input rail read from the onboard INA3221 via sysfs (in1_input mV x curr1_input mA), 5 Hz sampling, median over the stated window. 'Settled' samples were taken after 1-min load average dropped below 0.15. JetPack Ubuntu, Orin Nano Dev Kit carrier board, no display sleep forced, fan in default profile.

measurements.label: 15W desktop idle (2026-07-17, post-boot)
measurements.note: taken minutes after boot, desktop session up, SSH via USB gadget

measurements.label: 7W desktop idle (2026-07-17, post-boot)
measurements.note: taken ~5 min after reboot into 7W mode

measurements.label: 15W desktop idle (2026-07-18, mid-session)
measurements.note: anomalously low sample; same nominal state as the settled desktop measurement below, which read 0.56 W higher. Demonstrates untracked state dependence (suspects: fan state, network path, EMC/regulator state). Raw file was named idle_7w_desktop.json but the board was in 15W mode

measurements.label: 15W headless idle (settled, 2026-07-18)
measurements.note: graphical.target stopped; CPU 0%, GR3D 0%; rails: VDD_CPU_GPU_CV 0.481 W, VDD_SOC 1.165 W

measurements.label: 15W desktop idle (settled, 2026-07-18)
measurements.note: graphical.target restored, same LAN SSH path as headless sample; rails identical: VDD_CPU_GPU_CV 0.481 W, VDD_SOC 1.165 W

findings.headless_saving_note: Desktop vs headless is a null result: 3.698 vs 3.657 W (~1%), within the board's own idle wander. An idle desktop costs essentially nothing because the GPU rail-gates either way (GR3D 0%, identical per-rail readings).
findings.power_mode_saving_note: 7W mode saves ~0.16 W idle vs 15W while costing 37% of (unused) throughput.
findings.idle_floor_breakdown: At ~3.7 W elevated idle: VDD_SOC 1.17 W + VDD_CPU_GPU_CV 0.48 W; the remainder is dev-kit carrier overhead (regulators, PHYs, fan, USB) not addressable from software.
findings.variability_w: REVISED 2026-07-18 (later same day): the true deep-idle floor is 3.18 W, reproduced rock-stable across four 30 s samples during the FL benchmark session. The 3.66-3.74 W 'settled' readings above were an elevated post-activity state (fan hysteresis suspected), and the 3.14 W 'anomaly' was actually the floor. Idle therefore has two states ~0.5 W apart; sample after several minutes of true quiet to get the floor.
findings.conclusion: Software configuration cannot meaningfully reduce the Orin Nano Dev Kit idle floor (~3.6-3.7 W). For a flight/low-power deployment the levers are hardware: a custom carrier for the module, aggressive duty-cycling/deep sleep, or moving the (49-parameter, pure-Python-capable) classifier to a microcontroller-class device where idle is milliwatts.
